agent.name: Rachel Torres
agent.notable_actions: Offered 6-month price lock at current rate ($29.99); explained plan alternatives clearly; validated customer feedback on price increase communication and committed to passing it on

resolution.summary: Cancellation averted by applying 6-month price lock at $29.99. Root cause — 17% price increase to $34.99 — is not resolved; customer will face the full new price in 6 months. Customer expressed genuine concern about long-term affordability as a single parent. No conditional language on the save — customer can cancel anytime.

key_moments.description: Customer opens calmly but with immediate concern: 'Is that right?' — seeking explanation for price increase email
key_moments.trigger: Price increase email received; $5/month increase flagged

key_moments.description: Customer rejects generic 'continued investment' justification; discloses single-parent financial pressure
key_moments.trigger: Price increase feels unjustified without tangible new feature on March 1st

key_moments.description: Customer raises cancellation threat but softens it with emotional stake: family yoga Saturday routine with daughters
key_moments.trigger: Financial pressure vs emotional attachment to platform routine

key_moments.description: Customer accepts 6-month price lock: 'Six months at the old price? OK, that's something.'
key_moments.trigger: Concrete retention offer that delays the price impact

key_moments.description: Customer closes with constructive feedback and thanks agent warmly
key_moments.trigger: Agent handled with empathy; customer feels heard

pain_points.description: 17% price increase ($29.99 → $34.99) with no tangible new feature delivered on the change date. Customer is price-sensitive as a single parent managing household budget.
pain_points.verbatim_quote: I'm a single mum with two kids, I've got to watch every dollar. This might seem small to you but $5 a month adds up.

pain_points.description: Price increase communication framed as 'continued investment' with no specific near-term feature to justify the change. Customer explicitly called out the need for a concrete value demonstration.
pain_points.verbatim_quote: when you raise prices, you need to give people something tangible. Don't just say 'continued investment.' Show me a new feature I can use on March 1st that justifies the increase.

motivations.description: Family yoga routine with daughters on Saturday mornings — emotionally significant use case. Platform genuinely valued.
Price sensitivity is the primary pain trigger; offline downloads also cited as important utility feature.

feature_requests.feature: Offline downloads (already on Premium — mentioned as reason not to downgrade to Basic)

intent.primary: Cancel Premium subscription due to unannounced 17% price increase
intent.secondary: Seeking price relief or justification for the increase; willing to stay if cost pressure is addressed

factors: Price increase of 17% is the sole trigger — root cause unresolved
Price lock expires in 6 months; churn risk will resurface at month 20
No competitor alternatives mentioned — retention lever is price, not content gap

action_items.action: Apply 6-month price lock at $29.99 to account — completed in-call
Pass customer feedback on price increase communication to product team: customers need tangible feature announcement alongside price change
Flag account for proactive outreach in month 5 of price lock — before lock expires at month 20 of tenure
